refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability ≥ 0.999 (99.9%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the bidirectional command-and-telemetry link is operational to total operational time.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-010 addresses a critical operational requirement for remote equipment control. Refinement clarifies the measurement basis (bidirectional link), acceptance threshold (≥0.999), and evaluation scope (agreed period). High safety criticality demands explicit availability targets to prevent loss-of-control scenarios.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous monitoring of link state transitions (up/down) with timestamped logs; calculation of availability as (total_uptime / total_evaluation_period); comparison against 0.999 threshold; root-cause analysis of any downtime events.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall detect link loss within [X] milliseconds.
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall initiate safe state transition upon link loss.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall maintain network redundancy to achieve ≥0.999 availability.
FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall log and report link availability metrics to the Safety Services module.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Single communication link with passive monitoring and manual failover
DP-B: Dual-path network architecture with active link health monitoring and automatic failover to secondary path
DP-C: Mesh network topology with multi-hop redundancy and dynamic route optimization
DP-D: Hybrid approach combining dual-path primary/secondary with periodic health checks and graceful degradation
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Dual-path network architecture with active link health monitoring and automatic failover to secondary path

potential_sources: DP-B couples teleoperation link availability to network infrastructure design (primary/secondary paths must be physically/logically independent)
DP-B couples to Safety Services for alert generation upon link loss
DP-B couples to Vehicle control logic for safe state execution during failover
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are acceptable and necessary: dual-path architecture is the standard industrial approach for achieving 0.999+ availability in safety-critical teleoperation. Decoupling would require unrealistic single-path redundancy (e.g., DP-C mesh) with higher complexity and cost. DP-B is orthogonal to functional requirements for command execution and telemetry collection.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Two independent network paths (e.g., cellular + satellite, or dual cellular carriers) are available at the worksite.
Assumption: Failover latency is acceptable to Vehicle control logic (typically <100 ms for heavy equipment).
Risk: If both paths fail simultaneously, availability target cannot be met; mitigation requires geographic/provider diversity.
Risk: Active health monitoring adds computational overhead; must be validated against Vehicle processing budget.
Risk: Automatic failover logic must be deterministic and testable to meet safety criticality requirements.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly specify: (1) health check interval and timeout thresholds, (2) failover decision logic (e.g., majority voting, watchdog timer), (3) path independence criteria (e.g., different carriers, geographically separated antennas), (4) graceful degradation behavior during partial link loss.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Owns the dual-path network architecture, link health monitoring logic, automatic failover control, and telemetry/tasking routing. Interfaces with Vehicle for safe state commands and with Safety Services for link loss alerts.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands from tele-operator via primary or secondary network path; routes to Vehicle control logic.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits vehicle state, sensor data, and link health metrics from Vehicle to tele-operator via primary or secondary path.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Notifies Safety Services of link loss events, failover transitions, and availability metrics for real-time safety monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Manages primary and secondary network paths; monitors link state, initiates failover, and reports link availability to Teleoperation module.

module_containment_scores.notes: Teleoperation is the natural owner of dual-path architecture, link health monitoring, and failover logic. All DP-B elements (active monitoring, automatic failover, path management) are cohesive and belong in this module. Minimal diffusion: only Network Link interface is external.

Vehicle module owns vehicle control and actuation, not network infrastructure. Assigning DP-B to Vehicle would scatter network logic across vehicle firmware, creating tight coupling and diffusion. Not recommended.

Safety Services owns safety policy enforcement and alert generation, not network operations. Assigning DP-B to Safety Services would conflate network management with safety logic, violating separation of concerns. Safety Services should consume SafetyAlert from Teleoperation.
NGHE Solution is the top-level system container, not an implementation module for specific DPs. Assigning DP-B here would prevent proper architectural layering and ownership clarity.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the correct owner of DP-B (dual-path network architecture with active link health monitoring and automatic failover). This module naturally encapsulates network path management, link state monitoring, and failover decision logic. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and minimal, reducing diffusion risk.
containment_risks: Risk: If Network Link interface is not fully abstracted, Teleoperation may become tightly coupled to specific network hardware (e.g., modem drivers, antenna control). Mitigation: define Network Link as a clean abstraction layer.
Risk: Failover logic must be deterministic and testable; non-deterministic behavior could violate safety criticality. Mitigation: implement failover as a state machine with formal verification.
Risk: Teleoperation module may grow large if it also owns command/telemetry protocol encoding/decoding. Mitigation: separate protocol logic into a distinct sub-module if needed.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the boundary between Teleoperation and Network Link: Teleoperation owns failover decision logic and health monitoring; Network Link owns physical path management and low-level link state. Define a clean API between them (e.g., link_status_query(), switch_to_secondary_path()) to prevent diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (dual-path network architecture with active link health monitoring and automatic failover) directly addresses FR-NGHE-010 (achieve ≥0.999 teleoperation link availability). The DP is technically sound, well-established in industry, and measurable. No independence violations detected.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk: Teleoperation module is the natural owner of DP-B. Network Link interface is external and well-defined.
Moderate risk: Failover logic must be isolated from Vehicle control logic to prevent unintended coupling. Recommend clear API boundaries.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module achieves 0.95 containment score for DP-B. All core elements (dual-path management, health monitoring, failover control) are cohesive and belong in this module. External interfaces are minimal and necessary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High: Link availability is directly measurable via continuous monitoring of link state transitions. Acceptance criteria (≥0.999) is quantitative and testable. Teleoperation module can log link events and compute availability metrics.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear: FR-NGHE-010 is allocated to Teleoperation module. Sub-FRs (link loss detection, safe state transition, redundancy, metrics reporting) are traceable to Teleoperation and Safety Services. Ownership boundaries are explicit.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-B to specify health check interval, failover timeout, and path independence criteria (see selected_dp.recommended_revision).
Define Network Link interface contract explicitly (link_status_query, switch_to_secondary_path, etc.) to prevent diffusion.
Allocate sub-FRs to specific modules: FR-NGHE-010.1 and 010.2 to Teleoperation; FR-NGHE-010.3 to Teleoperation + Network Link; FR-NGHE-010.4 to Teleoperation + Safety Services.
Establish acceptance test plan for link availability measurement (e.g., 30-day continuous operation, dual-path failure injection tests).

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-010 is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The refined FR identifies four sub-FRs (link loss detection, safe state transition, redundancy, metrics reporting) that must be individually designed and allocated. DP-B is sound and well-allocated to Teleoperation, but sub-FRs require their own FR->DP->IM cycles to ensure completeness and traceability. High safety criticality demands rigorous decomposition of each sub-requirement.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall detect link loss within [X] milliseconds.
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall initiate safe state transition upon link loss.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall maintain network redundancy to achieve ≥0.999 availability.
FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall log and report link availability metrics to the Safety Services module.

assumptions: Two independent network paths (e.g., cellular + satellite, or dual carriers) are available at the worksite.
Failover latency <100 ms is acceptable to Vehicle control logic.
Active health monitoring overhead is within Vehicle processing budget.
Automatic failover logic can be implemented deterministically and tested to meet safety criticality.
Link availability is measured as uptime / total_evaluation_period over an agreed evaluation period (e.g., 30 days).
open_risks: Simultaneous failure of both network paths; mitigation requires geographic/provider diversity and risk acceptance by Safety Manager.
Failover logic non-determinism; mitigation requires formal verification and state machine design.
Network Link interface abstraction leakage; mitigation requires clear API contract.
Sub-FR allocation clarity; mitigation requires explicit ownership assignment in next decomposition level.
evidence_needed: Network path independence analysis (e.g., different carriers, geographically separated antennas).
Failover latency measurement and Vehicle control acceptance.
Health check interval and timeout threshold justification (e.g., based on worksite network conditions).
Acceptance test plan for link availability measurement (e.g., 30-day continuous operation, dual-path failure injection).
Safety case evidence for automatic failover logic (e.g., formal verification, FMEA, test coverage).
